docs.title: MapSnatch CLI
docs.text:
Open-source bulk export for MindMeister using the public API — no Business plan required.

pip install mapsnatch
mapsnatch --dry-run
  • Repository: github.com/gavinc/mapsnatch
  • Hosted product: mapsnatch.com (browser, one-time payment)
  • Support: SUPPORT.md

docs.title: Quick links
docs.text: Topic Page How the CLI is structured Architecture Formats and plan limits API limits Report bugs Issues

docs.title: MindMeister API limits
docs.text: MapSnatch only uses endpoints that work with a personal API token on standard plans. Behavior can change if Meister updates their API.

docs.title: Supported export formats
docs.text: Format Extension API path Notes FreeMind .mm /api/v2/maps/{id}.mm Zip container; recommended for portability PDF .pdf /api/v2/maps/{id}.pdf Raw PDF bytes MindMeister .mind /api/v2/maps/{id}.mind Zip container XMind .xmind /api/v2/maps/{id}.xmind Zip container RTF .rtf /api/v2/maps/{id}.rtf Text outline

docs.title: Not supported (CLI)
docs.text:
Format Why PNG / JPEG map_images endpoints often empty or plan-gated DOCX Empty responses observed on non-Business accounts

If you need Markdown or OPML, export as FreeMind (.mm) and convert with Pandoc or similar.

docs.title: Rate limiting
docs.text: The client sleeps 0.5s between export downloads by default to avoid hammering the API. Large libraries (hundreds of maps) can take several minutes.

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
• Token from MindMeister API settings
  • Sent as Authorization: Bearer <token>
  • Never commit tokens; revoke at Meister if leaked

docs.title: Business plan vs this tool
docs.text: MindMeister reserves bulk export in the product UI for Business. The public API still allows per-map export for authenticated users — MapSnatch automates that loop.

docs.title: Upstream references
docs.text:
• MindMeister API overview
  • Report API bugs to Meister; report MapSnatch CLI bugs on GitHub Issues

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
MapSnatch is a small Python CLI with three layers: CLI, client, exporter.

flowchart LR
  CLI[cli.py] --> Client[MindMeisterClient]
  CLI --> Exp[Exporter]
  Client --> API[MindMeister REST API]
  Exp --> FS[Output directory]

docs.title: meister_export.cli
docs.text:
• Parses arguments (--format, --output, --token, --dry-run)
  • Loads token from --token, MINDMEISTER_API_TOKEN, or .env via python-dotenv
  • Drives progress UI (tqdm) and exit codes

docs.title: meister_export.client.MindMeisterClient
docs.text:
• List maps: GET /services/rest/oauth2?method=mm.maps.getList (legacy REST, JSON)
  • Export map: GET /api/v2/maps/{id}.{format} (v2 API, binary)
  • Bearer token auth on a shared requests.Session
  • Optional rate_limit_delay (default 0.5s) between export calls

docs.title: meister_export.exporter.Exporter
docs.text:
• Iterates MapInfo list from the client
  • Writes files under --output with sanitised filenames
  • For zip-wrapped formats (mm, mind, xmind), extracts the correct member by extension

docs.title: Data flow
docs.text:
1. Authenticate with personal API token (never logged by the CLI).
  2. Fetch full map list once.
  3. For each map, download bytes for the chosen format.
  4. Save to disk; failures surface per-map without stopping the whole run (see tests for behavior).

docs.title: Non-goals
docs.text:
• No local database or cloud storage
  • No OAuth app flow (personal token only)
  • No modification of maps on MindMeister

docs.title: Related
docs.text: • Hosted web app (Stripe, zip in browser) is a separate codebase — same API concepts, different trust boundary.
